_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
This document covers the proposed CountEmissions EU Regulation (COM(2023) 441 final), which establishes a harmonised EU-wide methodology for calculating and disclosing greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions from freight and passenger transport services. The regulation is not yet formally adopted as of the date of this brief. Status is "close to adoption" following the TRAN committee vote in second reading on 17 March 2026.

**Convention notes:**
- "The workspace" refers throughout to a freight forwarder operating across air (primary), road (secondary), and ocean (tertiary) modes on Americas, Europe, and Asia trade lanes, handling live events, fine art, luxury goods, film and TV, high-value automotive, and humanitarian cargo.
- All claims are sourced inline. Items requiring legal review are labeled **Legal Confirmation Required**.
- The regulation is cited from COM(2023) 441 final and the European Parliament Legislative Train Schedule (as of 20 March 2026). The provisional agreement text of 2 December 2025 is referenced where the Legislative Train Schedule describes its content. The final Official Journal text is not yet published; analysis reflects the agreed provisional text as reported.

*Source: European Parliament Legislative Train Schedule, EU Framework for Harmonised Measurement of Transport and Logistics Emissions, 20 March 2026. https://www.europarl.europa.eu/legislative-train/spotlight-JD%2023-24/file-measurement-of-transport-and-logistics-emissions*

*Source: COM(2023) 441 final, European Commission, 11 July 2023. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=comnat:COM_2023_0441_FIN*

---
